live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it to the service
Orchestrates owner resolution, base record creation, routing derivation, and payload emission
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config defaults
Passes the resolved owner into storage as part of the base record
Persists only name, status, and owner in the base record
Builds routing_key from the already-resolved effective owner and item name
Computes routing_key from owner and name
Hands the stored record plus derived fields to the serializer for final emission
Emits the final payload by copying the record and attaching owner_source and routing_key

owner_source.derivation: Returns "explicit" when a non-blank owner argument is supplied, otherwise returns "default" alongside the configured default owner

routing_key.derivation: Called from sync_item after owner resolution; uses effective_owner and name

emission.derivation: Copies the base record and appends owner_source and routing_key without re-deriving them from storage

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner produces owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist
Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and labels owner_source=default
CLI forwards --owner through main() into the same service path and emits the same explicit-owner payload
Docs are expected to mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, and sync_item, which matches a documentation-surface issue rather than a storage bug

rejected_decoys.reason: Legacy helper is not called by the live sync path
Legacy routing helper is not used; live code calls serializer.build_routing_key from service
Unused helper; live path passes owner_source into serialize_payload directly
Rolled-back historical note that explicitly says it described a stale theory, not the live path
Unfinished draft speculation about storage becoming the source of truth
Future design intent only; does not describe the current implementation
